name: Workiva Financial Reporting API
what_it_does: Workiva REST API for enterprise financial reporting, compliance, and ESG disclosure platform. Enables AI agents to manage document workflows, import financial data into reports, trigger review and approval workflows, access audit trails, and integrate reporting data with ERP and BI systems. Workiva is used for SEC/EDGAR filings, ESG reporting, and regulatory disclosure by public companies.
use_cases: Importing financial data into reporting templates from close-to-report integration agents
Managing document review and approval workflows from financial reporting agents
Triggering SEC filing submissions from regulatory compliance agents
Accessing audit trails and evidence documentation from external audit integration agents
not_for: Private company reporting without SEC/regulatory filing requirements
Simple document management without connected data and workflows
SMB financial reporting without enterprise disclosure requirements
best_when: A public company CFO office using Workiva wants AI agents to automate data collection, validate reporting data, manage review workflows, and integrate financial close with SEC/ESG reporting.
avoid_when: FINANCIAL RISK: SEC filings and regulatory disclosures are legally binding. Errors in public financial reporting have severe legal and reputational consequences. Human review mandatory before filing.

auth.notes: Workiva uses OAuth 2.0 client credentials with workspace-scoped access. Scopes control access to documents, data, and admin functions. Enterprise customer API access via Workiva developer portal.

pricing.free_tier_limits: No free tier — enterprise financial reporting platform
pricing.paid_tiers: Enterprise annual contracts; pricing per solution (reporting, ESG, audit)
pricing.notes: Publicly traded (WK). Enterprise financial reporting used by 85%+ of Fortune 500. Developer portal with sandbox available for integration development.

performance.rate_limits: Rate limits per OAuth app; documented in developer portal

known_agent_gotchas: FINANCIAL RISK: SEC filings are legally binding — human review mandatory before any submission
No public MCP server — REST API with workspace-scoped OAuth2
Document hierarchy (workspace > folder > document) must be understood before integration
Data import to linked cells requires precise mapping to document structure
XBRL tagging for SEC filings has strict format requirements
ESG reporting frameworks (GRI, SASB, TCFD) have different data requirements
agent_readiness.security_notes: FINANCIAL RISK: SEC and ESG disclosures are legally binding. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR. OAuth2 with workspace scopes. Human review mandatory before regulatory submissions.

security_score_components.security_notes: SEC and ESG regulatory disclosure data. SOC2, ISO27001, GDPR. OAuth2 with workspace scopes. Legally binding filings require human review.
